L1.area: User-defined function registration and execution infrastructure

L2.description: A data race vulnerability exists in the auxiliary data API for user-defined functions. When multiple threads invoke the same custom function concurrently, one thread may obtain a direct reference to cached auxiliary data while another thread replaces it, causing the first thread's reference to dangle. This can lead to use-after-free and memory safety violations.

L3.area: SQLite user-defined scalar function context and its auxiliary data caching mechanism
L3.description: A data race vulnerability exists in the auxiliary data caching API for user-defined SQLite scalar functions. When multiple threads invoke the same custom function concurrently, one thread can obtain a direct reference to cached data stored via the context's auxdata methods while another thread's invocation replaces it, causing the reference to dangle. This violates memory safety due to the lack of atomic reference counting or synchronization on the stored auxiliary data.

ground_truth.reason: The get_aux and set_aux methods on the scalar function Context allow concurrent access to auxiliary data without synchronization, enabling a data race where one thread reads a reference while another deallocates and replaces the underlying data.
